feed.title: SAP Community - SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
feed.subtitle: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Import Story Report from Content Store Faied
entry.content:
I tried importing a Story Report from the Content Store but it failed with this error "Error: This report has been imported previously. A Story with the same ID already exists in the system. Existing report name: SF_XM_My_Team_Dashboard_v2.0"

I tried importing with and without selecting "Selecting this checkbox will overwrite the previously imported Story" option but both failed.

Anyone know how I can find this report in my system?

entry.title: Hide Award Configuration Fields from Workflow Approval Page of Reward and Recognition
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

 

Issue:

When a Spot Award is initiated by the nominator, it goes through multiple approvers in the workflow. During this process, approvers can view additional details such as the Budget Holder and Spot Award Program Details by clicking the related button.

Currently, this visibility cannot be controlled using Permissions or MDF configurations, as these settings are required for both the Nominee and Nominator roles.

 

Question:

Is there any way to hide the Budget Holder and Spot Award Program Details from being visible on the workflow approval page, without impacting the required access for the Nominee and Nominator?

@xavierlegarrec - Please suggest

entry.title: Change audit for workflows?
entry.content:
Hi SF Community,

Is anyone aware of change audit reporting on SuccessFactors workflows?   There does not seem to be anything built into the system - the only information available in the UI is the date/time of the most recent change, and the name of the person who made the change.

There's no information on what changed at that time, or any history of previous changes.

Lacking any system audit, has anyone found a manual method that satisfies your internal and external audit team?

Thanks in advance for any info you can share!

Ricardo Rojas

entry.title: Problems with Hands on practice SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Position Management
entry.content: @Gopinath_Murugaiyan Hi! I was wondering if there is any technical problem with the hands-on practice for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Position Management THR79? I'm trying to complete it before the first certification attempt, but it's not working. Appreciating that you can help me. Thanks!

entry.title: How can I delete a termination action via bulk upload in EC?
entry.content: Please walk me through the step-by-step process for deleting a termination action in EC via bulk upload?

entry.title: Fields in position object couldn't be replaced with "NULL" value in SuccessFactors RCM/ONB
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I am currently supporting an AMS for SuccessFactors RCM, ONB 2.0, and EC. We have run into a technical deadlock regarding data propagation during the Internal Hire (IJP / Crossboarding) process when moving an employee between positions with different Org Layer depths.

The Business Scenario: An internal employee is transferring from:

  • Old Position: Has Org structure down to the Section layer (Department: A -> Section: X).

  • New Position: Org structure stops at the Department layer (Department: B -> Section: NULL / Blank).

The Integration Setup: In Recruit-to-Hire Data Mapping, the EC field jobInfo.section is mapped directly to the Job Requisition's Section field (which is legitimately blank/null on the Requisition).

The Symptom: 

in QAS -> When initiating Onboarding for this candidate, the payload fails at the very first step (Validate HRIS data for user) with the following error:

"jobInfo : Section '40000065' is not associated with Department '30000314'. Check the Section data object configuration and enter a valid Department."

in PRD -> Initiate Onboarding were working fine with no issue. Although, after the Actions has been created with expected event reason then value in Section field from previous position still there while it should be "NULL".

Our Investigation so far:

  1. The system correctly passes all fields from the Job Req. However, it refuses to pass the NULL value for the Section in Initiate Onboarding step. Instead, it retains the employee's legacy Section (40000065), which causes an MDF Parent-Child association violation while initiating onboarding.

  2. The configuration for Recruit to Hire Data Mapping and Business Rules are correctly set up.

Our Questions to the Experts:

  1. How is this standard scenario (moving from a "deep" org layer to a "shallow" org layer) intended to be handled in SuccessFactors without breaking MDF associations?

  2. What are workarounds to fix this kind of issue? I have check all configuration I can think about in PRD and QAS are the same.

Any insights, workarounds, or shared battle scars regarding this scenario would be immensely appreciated!

Thank you,

entry.title: Username, IAS, business and personal email addresses recommendations
entry.content:
Dear Community, 

we are facing a recurring situation with one of our customers. They would like some employees to be able to access to Successfactors through IAS but these employees don't have a business email address. 

Therefore we would want them to access with their personal email address. 

According to this KBA, mapping the business email address to the username is not recommended, although it can be done. 

We thought of creating a third email type (login email), and feed it via rules or integration: if no business email, feed it with the personal email address; otherwise feed it with the business email. Then map this email to the username, and send it to IAS to the LOGIN NAME. 

My question here is - what are SAP recommendations for those scenarios? I've been doing some unsuccessful research - this is not the first time we work with a company whose employees could not have a business email address. 

Thank you for your help

Regards

Laura

entry.title: Restore True Time-Based Scaling in Compensation History Graph within Latest People Profile
entry.content:
In SAP SuccessFactors Employee Profile, the Compensation History graph available in the Latest People Profile displays salary change events with equal spacing between data points, regardless of the actual time elapsed between the corresponding effective dates.

As a result, salary changes that occurred several years apart are displayed with the same visual distance as changes that occurred only a few months apart.

Business Challenge

The current visualization does not accurately represent the timeline of compensation evolution. Users reviewing an employee's salary history cannot immediately understand the duration between compensation changes, as the graph treats the timeline as a sequence of events rather than a date-based scale.

This behavior differs from the previous profile experience, where data points were positioned proportionally according to their effective dates, providing a more accurate and intuitive representation of salary progression over time.

For example:

  • A salary change between January 2019 and December 2020 should appear significantly farther apart than a change between December 2020 and March 2021.
  • In the current Latest People Profile, both intervals are displayed with identical spacing.

Requested Enhancement

Introduce an option, or restore the previous standard behavior, so that compensation history graphs in the Latest People Profile use a true date-based timeline.

Data points should be positioned proportionally according to their effective dates, ensuring that visual distances accurately reflect the actual time elapsed between compensation events.

Business Value

  • Improved readability and interpretation of compensation history.
  • More accurate visualization of employee salary progression.
  • Better support for HR business partners, managers, and compensation specialists during salary reviews.
  • Consistency with the behavior previously available in the legacy profile experience.
  • Enhanced user experience and data transparency.

Expected Benefit

Users will be able to immediately understand both the magnitude and timing of salary changes without needing to manually analyze individual effective dates. This will improve decision-making during compensation reviews, talent discussions, and employee history analysis.

entry.title: AI Powered Assistance in SAP Learning Hub
entry.content:
Hi There

I have recently noticed that there is a button "Access AI Learning" on the SAP SuccessFactors learning journeys in SAP Learning Hub. 

Please provide more details on how this feature can be used.

Is there a cost involved if we start consuming this service while going through the learning journey?

entry.title: lease explain how to change the termination date through bulk upload in EC
entry.content: Could someone explain how termination dates can be changed for larger employees through bulk upload in EC?

entry.title: Productized integrations (Master Data Sync / Hire Sync) when EC is not master
entry.content:
Hello SAP community,
 
I have a question regarding the productized integrations between SAP SuccessFactors (Employee Central / Recruiting) and SmartRecruiters, specifically around Master Data Sync and Hire Sync.
 
In several of my current customer contexts, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central is not positioned as the master system, but rather as a “slave” system, or functional use
 
In this setup, I would like to understand:
 
- Are the standard integrations (Master Data Sync and Hire Sync) fully supported when EC is not the system of record?
- Are there any known limitations or constraints in such architectures?
- What are the recommended patterns or best practices for these integrations when EC is not the master system?
Any feedback or experience on this type of setup would be greatly appreciated.
Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Limitations with Business Rule - Benefit Employee Claim SuccessFactors
entry.content:
This is being added in the Q/A Section to grab the attention of the Product Experts and the Engineering Team. Please find the issue described in detailed below

So, One of my Clients wanted to have the petty cash process automated through the SuccessFactors and We went ahead with the Benefit Employee Claim - Reimbursement Type. There are two categories of Reimbursement, Petty Cash Advance and Petty Cash Expense. Petty Cash Advance is applied first against which Petty Cash Expense is later raised once the expenses are done. Unique Reference numbers are generated for both Petty Cash Advance and Expenses for tracking and reporting purposes. When Employees raise Petty Cash Expense against advance received , they are required to enter the Petty Cash Advance Reference Number mandatorily. To make the proces efficient , convenient and error - free , we automated the petty cash advance reference number  by auto population (User selects the date on which the petty cash advance was raised) and reference number is auto-populated in the request. This was done using a Lookup Rule.

Adding more context to this , This is a multiple proof yes Benefit (Benefit Employee Claim Detail Object is being used) to allow the employees to book expenses under multiple categories and Total Amount is populated and is a ready only field (multiple proof yes makes the total amount field read only).This is where the issues arises, where SAP has claimed that any rule triggered after total amount field is filled would make the total amount field reset to 0 (Only in the UI) , however it is fine in the UI's and amount claimed gets added to the accumulation Object. This is a very confusing for the end - users as they would mistake the 0 , not having this rule would make the process tiresome as they have to manually enter the request number which is inconvenient for the end users and is error-prone. Attaching screenshot of the rule below

Hoping someone from the Product Engineering Team works on this and get this resolved as rule triggers after total amount resetting the field to 0 is a concerning limitation.I am open for any discussions on this !

entry.title: SuccessFactors Entra Integration - Respecting employee's time zone
entry.content:
Hi Community,

We are in the middle of configuring the SuccessFactors / Microsoft Entra (Azure) integration but we are facing an issue with some of our global teams where termination events are occurring in our local tenant time zone (Australian Eastern Time) which is revoking access too early for some teams in later time zones. We have a global workforce and have not been able to find a simple solution to ensure accounts are created and disabled at the right time based on the employee's location

Has anyone found a solution either within SuccessFactors or Entra that helps offset the time when account is created or disabled based on the user's time zone without the need for an additional API?

Thanks!

entry.title: Document Generation – Temporary vs. Home Address Logic
entry.content:
Hi all,

I would kindly ask for your advice regarding Document Generation in SuccessFactors.

The requirement from the client is that in the contract the temporary address should be printed if it exists; otherwise, the home address should be used.

I am using Document Generation with a mapping rule, and I tried to build a business rule to return only one address to the template. However, I am facing issues due to the fact that Address Information is a multi-record object, and the mapping rule:

  • processes each address record separately,
  • does not support proper filtering (for example, “get first record where address type = temporary”),
  • and as a result I either:
    • get multiple values error, or
    • always get the home address instead of temporary, depending on how the rule is structured.

Due to project constraints, I cannot use:

  • Employee Central Business Rules for pre-calculating the address, or
  • advanced template logic (loops/conditions) in the document template.

Has anyone faced a similar limitation and found a reliable workaround within Document Generation Mapping Rules only, that ensures:

  • only one address is returned, and
  • preference is given to the temporary address if it exists?

Any advice or best practice would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance, Urška

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central - Employee Profile

entry.title: Blog post awaiting review for more than 5 business days — no update received.
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I have submitted a SAP Blog on 27th on June, more than 5 days ago, so far did not receive any update on the approval. Can you all please help me figure out how much days does it take generally ?

Thanks

entry.title: Overtimes to more time accounts
entry.content:
Hello experts,

@Frans_Smolders @Volker_Ruof 

I have a requirement from a client regarding the SuccessFactors Time Management setup. They need overtime hours exceeding 42 hours per week to be transferred to specific time accounts from which employees can later take time off (Time Off in Lieu).

The business logic for the weekly distribution is as follows:

  • Hours between 42h and 43h should be posted to the Flextime account.

  • Hours between 43h and 45h should be posted to the Overtime account.

  • Any hours above 45h should be posted to the Extrahours account.

Is there a standard solution or a best practice configuration to achieve this within Time Valuation?"

Thanks a lot

Jan

entry.title: Known Limitation in Timesheet Approval Center – "Approve All" Button
entry.content:
We enabled the Timesheet Approval Center for one of our clients and, during validation, identified that the "Approve All" button remains enabled when "All Status" is selected, irrespective of the actual timesheet statuses. In contrast, the functionality behaves as expected when a specific status is selected.

 

I highlighted this behavior to the SAP team for review. After investigating the issue, SAP confirmed that this is a product limitation rather than a configuration issue. Based on the issue we raised, SAP has now published a Knowledge Base Article (KBA) documenting this limitation.

https://me.sap.com/notes/3776833

This confirms that the observed behavior is an existing product limitation and not an issue specific to configuration.

entry.title: Attachment/Document view per legal entity in Manage Documents
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Is it now possible to have separation on attachment view or documents in document view per legal entity?

Is there a workaround for this?

As per this KBA, 2789197 - Attached files on Manage Documents - SuccessFactors Platform - SAP for Me  it is not possible, but we want to confirm and look for workaround.

 

Many thanks!

 

Best Regards,

Gelo

entry.title: SuccessFactors SOC Reports 2026 (types 1 and 2)
entry.content:
Hi guys,

I'm from HRIS, and currently an EC Superadmin for a SAP SF instance, hosted in Frankfurt.

The Audit team is requesting 2026's SOC Reports Type 1 and Type 2.
Could you point me in the direction where to get the right and updated one for 2026?

I saw this: https://www.sap.com/about/trust-center/certification-compliance/2025-h1-central-cloud-services-c5-report.html
But when you display the List of Services, Successfactors is not mentioned.

entry.title: Display the leave approvers in absence form
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am working one of the Saudi Based project. We have absence form for vacation leave where the client requires to get display the workflow approves in the form. Appreciated if you can let me know what should be done and great helpful if anyone come across and implemented this in your past projects.

Thanks,

Keshav Reddy G.
